description.task: Implement a LinearSearch class with methods for: (1) basic linear search returning index or -1, (2) find all occurrences of a target, and (3) find the maximum element with its index.
instructions: search: for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++) if(arr[i]==target) return i;
Linear search is O(n) — must check every element in the worst case
arr={4,2,7,2,9,2,1}: 7 is at index 2; 2 appears at [1,3,5]; max is 9 at index 4
description.hint: search: for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++) if(arr[i]==target) return i; findAll: indices.add(i); findMax: if(arr[i]>maxVal){maxVal=arr[i];maxIdx=i;}
description.expectedOutput:
Search 7: index 2
Search 5: index -1
All 2s: [1, 3, 5]
Max: 9 at index 4

description.task: Implement Sentinel Linear Search which places the target at the end of the array to eliminate the bounds-check (i >= n) from the inner loop. This reduces constant-factor overhead. Compare with regular linear search.
instructions: After loop: if(i<n) return i; else return -1;
Sentinel at arr[n] guarantees the loop always terminates (avoids out-of-bounds)
arr={15,3,8,22,5,17,11}: 17 is at index 5; 99 is not in original (i==n after loop)
Sentinel optimization saves one comparison per iteration — useful for very large arrays
description.expectedOutput:
Regular  search 17: 5
Sentinel search 17: 5
Regular  search 99: -1
Sentinel search 99: -1

description.task: Implement linear search for a 2D matrix. Search every cell row by row and column by column. Return the {row, col} position of the target, or {-1, -1} if not found. Also count how many cells were examined.
instructions: 13 is at row=1, col=2 (0-indexed) — visited 7 cells to reach it
99 is not in the matrix — all 12 cells visited
Linear search on a 2D m×n matrix is O(m*n)
description.hint: search: if(matrix[row][col]==target) return new int[]{row,col}; countSearch: visited++; if(matrix[row][col]==target) return visited;
description.expectedOutput:
13 found at: [1, 2]
Cells visited for 13: 7
99 found at: [-1, -1]
Cells visited for 99: 12
